title: Space Tech Briefing — June 05, 2026
summary:
Good morning, space enthusiasts and future voyagers! This is your daily dose of cosmic currents, live from the Space Technology Briefing, and it is Friday, June 05, 2026. Get ready to launch into the latest and greatest from beyond our blue planet!

As we wait for today's new launches and discoveries, let's take a moment to look back at a major recent development that has everyone in the space community talking. Just earlier this week, we heard thrilling news for European space exploration. France is set to maintain a strong presence in low Earth orbit, thanks to a landmark agreement with the American space company Vast.

The big announcement confirmed that two prominent French astronauts, Thomas Pesquet and Arnaud Prost, are slated for private space missions in 2027! This is truly exciting. Thomas Pesquet, a familiar face to many, is expected to join the International Space Station aboard Vast's very first private mission. Meanwhile, Arnaud Prost will make history by participating in the inaugural mission to Vast’s brand-new private space station, Haven-1.

This pivotal agreement, unveiled during the prestigious Choose France summit, underscores a powerful partnership. It allows France to keep its astronauts active in orbit while collaborating on a pioneering private space station project. The French President himself confirmed this groundbreaking deal, highlighting the nation's commitment to the future of commercial spaceflight.

It’s a fantastic step forward, ensuring that French expertise continues to contribute to human spaceflight, now in partnership with the rapidly expanding private sector. We'll be keeping a close eye on these missions as 2027 approaches!

That's it for our morning briefing today. Thank you for tuning in and staying connected to the ever-expanding universe of space technology. Keep looking up, and have a stellar day!
